title: 6 Key Takeaways from Salesforce’s Trailblazing Women London
author: Salesforce Careers

sections.heading: Introduction
sections.text: We came. We saw. And we blazed some incredible trails at our fifth annual gender equality summit,Trailblazing Women. This landmark event — hosted in London for the first time ever! — is part of Salesforce’s ongoing commitment to advancing gender equality, and featured an exceptional lineup of change-making women leaders, including our keynote speaker: activist, actress, and host Jameela Jamil. From promoting diversity in leadership to highlighting the importance of equitable practices in the workplace, let’s take a look at some of the most transformative moments and key highlights of this event:

sections.heading: 1. Seek challenges to unlock growth.
sections.text: Run towards the difficult opportunities. Take the hard path, you will never regret it. Take the roles other people don’t want to do. Take the roles that don’t immediately seem attractive, they are the key to the door because you can make the most of them.” Run towards the difficult opportunities. Take the hard path, you will never regret it. Take the roles other people don’t want to do. Take the roles that don’t immediately seem attractive, they are the key to the door because you can make the most of them.”

sections.heading: 2. Celebrate your successes and empower your team.
sections.text: If you’re not comfortable shouting about your own successes, find a way to do it appropriately … be more braggy because you do your team a disservice when you don’t.” If you’re not comfortable shouting about your own successes, find a way to do it appropriately … be more braggy because you do your team a disservice when you don’t.”

sections.heading: 3. Do not fear failure. Instead, unleash your potential.
sections.text: All of the best learning, the fundamental learning we will ever do comes from our mistakes. It is vital to our evolution to make mistakes and then grow and learn from them.” All of the best learning, the fundamental learning we will ever do comes from our mistakes. It is vital to our evolution to make mistakes and then grow and learn from them.”

sections.heading: 4. Expand your horizons and engage in diverse spaces.
sections.text: In this world of constant change and constant learning, it’s very rare that you will have someone that you learn from and they can’t learn anything from you.” In this world of constant change and constant learning, it’s very rare that you will have someone that you learn from and they can’t learn anything from you.” My life is hugely enriched by the diversity of the people that I spend my time with, and I don’t want to exist in an echo chamber of people exactly like me because it would mean my humanity would stop growing.” My life is hugely enriched by the diversity of the people that I spend my time with, and I don’t want to exist in an echo chamber of people exactly like me because it would mean my humanity would stop growing.”

sections.heading: 5. Lean into your identity.
sections.text: I know what I’m good at now. I know what I’m not so good at, and I defer to my team for those kinds of things. I don’t have to be everything for everyone all of the time anymore.” I know what I’m good at now. I know what I’m not so good at, and I defer to my team for those kinds of things. I don’t have to be everything for everyone all of the time anymore.”

sections.heading: 6. Embrace your differences.
sections.text: I don’t want anybody to try to fit in. I want everybody to rock their beautiful differences, find their voice, to belong uniquely as themselves, but to have collective grace for difference.” I don’t want anybody to try to fit in. I want everybody to rock their beautiful differences, find their voice, to belong uniquely as themselves, but to have collective grace for difference.” Want to catch all the action? Tune into the full recording of the 2023 Trailblazing Women Summithere!

existing_ctas.heading: Blaze your trail to Salesforce!
existing_ctas.body: At Salesforce, we’re not just shaping the future. We’re turning big ideas into breakthroughs. Want to join the #1 AI CRM that’s leading the digital labor revolution? Explore our open roles today!

existing_ctas.heading: We can’t wait to meet you!
existing_ctas.body: Join our Talent Community and be the first to know about open roles, career tips, events happening near you, and much more.

raw_html:
We came. We saw. And we blazed some incredible trails at our fifth annual gender equality summit, Trailblazing Women.

This landmark event — hosted in London for the first time ever! — is part of Salesforce’s ongoing commitment to advancing gender equality, and featured an exceptional lineup of change-making women leaders, including our keynote speaker: activist, actress, and host Jameela Jamil.

Blaze your trail to Salesforce!

At Salesforce, we’re not just shaping the future. We’re turning big ideas into breakthroughs. Want to join the #1 AI CRM that’s leading the digital labor revolution? Explore our open roles today!

Apply now

From promoting diversity in leadership to highlighting the importance of equitable practices in the workplace, let’s take a look at some of the most transformative moments and key highlights of this event:

1. Seek challenges to unlock growth.

2. Celebrate your successes and empower your team.

3. Do not fear failure. Instead, unleash your potential.

4. Expand your horizons and engage in diverse spaces.

5. Lean into your identity.

6. Embrace your differences.

Want to catch all the action? Tune into the full recording of the 2023 Trailblazing Women Summit here!

We can’t wait to meet you!

Join our Talent Community and be the first to know about open roles, career tips, events happening near you, and much more.

Join today
